description:
During active balancing:
  · Selected cells shall be discharged through balancing resistor
  · Balancing shall continue until delta < 20 mV or timeout reached
  · Balancing shall be suspended when pack current > 5 A

Upon charger connection detection (CP/PP or CC signal):
  · Verify all safety preconditions
  · IF all preconditions satisfied
    THEN BMS_ChargeReady_Flag := 1
  · ELSE BMS_ChargeReady_Flag := 0, report reason on CAN

While BMS_CellOT_Flag = 1 AND charging:
  · IF cell temperature > 45 C
    THEN BMS_ChargeDerating_Active := 1
  · Charge current shall be reduced proportionally to temperature rise

A charge session shall be terminated on any of the following conditions:
  · BMS_ChargeComplete_Flag = 1
  · BMS_ChargeTerm_Req = 1 (from section 1 overvoltage)
  · Any active fault requiring contactor open (sections 1-8)
  · Charger disconnected

IF  any cell voltage <= 2.80 V
THEN
  · BMS_CellUV_Flag := 1
  · Limit discharge power

The BMS shall continuously track:
  · Capacity fade (current vs. rated capacity)
  · DC internal resistance trend
  · Cell voltage deviation under load

Upon VCU_HVDisconnect_Req or critical fault:
  · Open both main contactors
  · Discharge DC link capacitance
  · BMS_SystemState := HV_Deactivating
  · THEN Standby or SafeState as appropriate

While BMS_CANFault_Flag = 1:
  · Essential sensor inputs shall continue to be monitored
  · Basic safety functions shall be maintained
  · IF CAN communication restored AND fault cleared
    THEN resume normal operation

The following data shall be logged for each balancing event:
  · Balancing start and end timestamps
  · Cells balanced and discharge duration per cell
  · Cell voltage delta before and after balancing

IF  BMS_CellOV_Detect = 1 for a continuous duration >= t_CellOV_Debounce
THEN
  · BMS_CellOV_Flag := 1
  · Store the maximum cell voltage and affected cell ID in the fault record

Wake-up sources:
  · CAN bus activity
  · Dedicated wake-up pin
Wake-up shall complete initialisation within 500 ms and report BMS_SystemState on CAN.

IF  calculated SOH <= 80 %
THEN
  · BMS_BatteryDegrade_Warn := 1
  · Store DTC P0A0F in non-volatile memory

Upon BMS_ChargeComplete_Flag = 1:
  · BMS_ChargeTerm_Req := 1
  · Command charge contactor Open
  · Store charge event record (final SOC, energy, duration)

IF  pack discharge current >= 300 A for >= 10 ms
THEN
  · BMS_SCD_Flag := 1
  · Open both main contactors immediately
  · BMS_SystemState := SafeState

While BMS_Fault_Latch = 1:
  · All contactor closure prohibited
  · Normal operation blocked until power cycle
  · Fault code readable via diagnostic interface

The following data shall be stored in the fault record upon BMS_CellOV_Flag = 1:
  · Peak cell voltage and affected cell ID
  · Pack temperature at time of fault
  · Event timestamp

Recovery from overvoltage safe state:
IF  BMS_SystemState = SafeState AND all cell voltages < 4.00 V for >= 5 s
THEN
  · BMS_OV_RecoverAllow := 1

IF  BMS_ChargeTerm_Req = 1 AND any cell voltage >= r_CellOV_L3_Threshold
THEN
  · BMS_CellOV_L3_Flag := 1

IF  any cell temperature <= low temperature limit
THEN
  · BMS_CellUT_Flag := 1
  · BMS_ChargeReady_Flag := 0 (charging prohibited)

IF  cumulative charge throughput exceeds design cycle limit
THEN
  · BMS_SOHEstFault_Flag := 1
  · Trigger SOH re-calibration request
